title: Bristol Outdoor Lounge Chair
summary: A boxed-seat lounge chair with an angled back frame and wide flat arms — build the seat box first, then add a genuinely reclined back for real outdoor lounging.
description:
This lounge chair builds its comfort into the frame geometry rather than into cushions alone: the front and back seat frames form a simple box first, and only once that box exists does the back frame attach at a real angle, cut into the bottom of the back frame itself rather than achieved with hardware or a hinge.

Clamp every joint while it dries — the plan calls this out specifically for the smoothest results, since a slightly proud joint on outdoor furniture both looks rough and catches water. The back frame is genuinely flimsy on its own right after attaching; that's expected and temporary. The wide flat arms are what stabilize it for good, tying the back frame to the seat box and turning a wobbly assembly into a rigid chair.

Cleats support the seat slats and can be mounted straight across or angled slightly downward and backward for extra reclined comfort — a real, easy-to-make choice with no wrong answer. Seat slats go on last, followed by cushions if you want them; the frame alone is comfortable enough for many people without added padding.

cutList.note: Longest point measurement, one end cut at a 15° bevel.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at a 15° bevel.

steps.title: Build the back and front frames
steps.body: Build the seat box back frame and front frame separately, clamping all joints while the glue sets for the smoothest results.

steps.title: Join the seat box
steps.body: Attach the front and back frames together to form the seat box.

steps.title: Build the angled back frame
steps.body: Build the back frame with its angled bottom cuts, which create the chair's reclined back angle.

steps.title: Attach the back and arms
steps.body: Attach the back frame to the seat box — it will be flimsy at this stage, which is expected. Attach the arms, which tie the back frame to the seat box and add real rigidity.

steps.title: Add the cleats and seat slats
steps.body: Attach the small cleats, angling them downward and backward slightly if you want a more reclined feel. Add the seat slats across the cleats.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand thoroughly, apply an exterior sealer or stain, and add cushions if desired.

unresolvedImages.alt: Outdoor lounge chair with a reclined back and wide flat arms
